articles.title: HOW AI IS BLURRING THE LINES BETWEEN PRODUCT AND CX TEAMS
articles.summary: Product-CX relationships break down due to misaligned goals and poor communication. AI is changing this dynamic – creating new opportunities for collaboration that smart product teams are already capitalizing on.

articles.title: BATTERIES INCLUDED, OPINIONS REQUIRED: THE SPECIALIZATION OF APP GEN PLATFORMS
articles.summary: AI app-generation platforms aren't competing in a zero-sum game - they're thriving by specializing in niches like prototyping, personal tools, and production apps. Like foundation models, they grow through complementary strengths, not direct competition.

articles.title: AI'S "JUST SHIP IT." PROBLEM
articles.summary: AI significantly boosts productivity for simpler tasks but offers minimal benefits for complex work, leading companies to mistakenly overestimate AI's impact on overall efficiency. True productivity gains depend heavily on ideation and validation, processes AI can't substantially improve.

articles.title: SUPERHUMAN: WHY GOOD BEATS PERFECT IN SURVEY DESIGN
articles.summary: Companies often sabotage user surveys by demanding overly precise answers, causing users to abandon them. Simplifying questions, removing cumbersome dropdowns, and clearly indicating progress boosts completion rates and gathers better insights.

articles.title: PARADIGM SHIFTS AND THE WINNER'S CURSE
articles.summary: Success in one era can block vision in the next. Apple and Amazon risk missing the AI shift, while Google may thrive by staying adaptable.

articles.title: ARE YOU PRICING FOR FEATURES, ACCESS, OR OUTCOMES?
articles.summary: AI is changing the basic definitions of what software is, what it can do, and how it delivers value. Pricing models designed around gating features or access might no longer be fit for purpose. Read _From Features to Outcomes: The 3 Eras of Software Value_ [9]on the Metronome blog [9].

articles.title: THE REWARD IS...MORE WORK
articles.summary: PMs often get stuck in ops after early success makes them indispensable, limiting focus on growth. To escape, they should productize repeatable tasks, set transition boundaries, and position ops maturity as key to scale.

articles.title: THE BEST VALIDATION HAPPENS BEFORE YOU WRITE A SINGLE LINE OF CODE
articles.summary: Testing assumptions early protects a PM's credibility and avoids costly mistakes by replacing guesswork with evidence. Simple tools like prototypes, fake-door tests, and interviews validate ideas fast.

articles.title: HOW TO BE AN EMPATHETIC MANAGER WITHOUT BECOMING A THERAPIST
articles.summary: Lead with empathy, not therapy. Listen well, guide toward action, and keep emotional reactions in check.

articles.title: ARE WE READY FOR AI SALES REPS?
articles.summary: Replacing human sales-assist with an AI avatar worked surprisingly well for RB2B. Results stayed steady, costs dropped, and great documentation made it all possible.

articles.title: POOR CONTEXT IS HOLDING BACK YOUR PROMPT
articles.summary: Product marketers must avoid weak, polluted, or conflicting context, instead pairing prompt templates with concise, targeted context templates aligned specifically to each use case.

articles.title: DON'T CHASE FUNCTIONALITY NEAR THE CORE
articles.summary: Rather than competing directly with dominant AI platforms, successful GPT startups carve out niches these models can't or won't address.
